author: HWY1dot0
description: Capture the currently selected content as a PNG and copy it to the clipboard. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: HWY1dot0
manifest.description: Capture selected note content as a PNG. Copies to clipboard when possible, with a vault file fallback on mobile.
manifest.name: Screenshot Selection

name: Screenshot Selection

releases.author: github-actions[bot]
releases.description: See the changelog for details on 0.2.10.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description:
Fix mobile content being wrong (0.2.8 regression follow-up).

0.2.8 still rasterized blank on iOS and fell back to the text renderer, because it re-rendered the selection with MarkdownRenderer into an offscreen subtree — which iOS WKWebView cannot rasterize. 0.2.1 worked because it cloned the live, already-rendered Reading-view DOM.

- **Mobile now clones the live rendered DOM** (range.cloneContents) first; the MarkdownRenderer re-render is only a fallback.
- **Selection no longer lost on tap:** the last in-note selection is stashed continuously, so opening the menu/ribbon doesn't drop it.
- **No false 'iOS could not rasterize':** blank detection now scans at full resolution instead of downscaling, which had wrongly flagged thin text as blank.

Best results in Reading view.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description:
Mobile capture fixes (the real render works on iOS — 0.2.1 proved it, then 0.2.6 broke it).

- **Content correct again.** 0.2.6 had replaced the real themed rendering with a text-only canvas fallback, so mobile captures looked nothing like the note. Mobile now rasterizes the real themed DOM first.
- **Blank-only fallback.** The text fallback now fires only when iOS actually returns a blank canvas (pixel-sampled), not unconditionally.
- **No more trailing blank strip.** Whitespace-trimming now runs on mobile too (it had been desktop-only since 0.2.2).
- **Visible diagnostics.** The capture notice shows whether the themed render or the text fallback was used.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Fixes mobile captures choosing the wrong content. Mobile capture now prefers explicit editor selection, then rendered DOM selection, then current editor block, instead of letting the current block override a rendered selection.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Adds a mobile canvas fallback renderer for selected markdown/current block captures. This bypasses DOM-to-image rendering on iOS WebView, which was producing blank screenshots, while keeping desktop DOM capture unchanged.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Attempts to fix blank iOS captures by rendering the capture node visibly in the viewport before export and by generating the PNG before writing to the clipboard instead of using promise-style ClipboardItem data.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Avoids blank captures on iOS by keeping the mobile capture node inside the viewport, skipping forced content-height trimming on mobile, and waiting one animation frame before rendering the PNG.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Fixes mobile capture cases where opening the editor menu or command UI collapses the current selection. Editor-menu captures now snapshot selected markdown/current block before iOS can clear it, and mobile block capture no longer depends on the MarkdownView mode string.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Tightens mobile screenshot captures by trimming extra rendered whitespace, avoiding slow whole-view fallback captures on mobile, lowering mobile render scale, shortening mobile asset waits, and showing immediate capture feedback.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Improves the mobile screenshot workflow: mobile capture now tries to copy the PNG to the clipboard first, falls back to saving in the vault if iOS rejects clipboard image writing, and adds faster camera/ribbon and editor-menu entry points.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Adds mobile capture-to-file support while preserving desktop clipboard capture and optional watermark settings.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description:
## New: optional watermark

You can now stamp a watermark onto each capture. It's **off by default** — nothing changes unless you turn it on in *Settings → Screenshot Selection*.

- Two styles: a **corner label**, or a **diagonal tiled** overlay across the image
- Configurable text, opacity, font size, color, and (for the corner style) which corner
- Drawn onto the image at capture time, so it stays on the PNG you paste

Everything else works as before. Update via Community Plugins, or download `main.js`, `manifest.json`, and `styles.css` below.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Adds `fundingUrl` to manifest so Obsidian shows a Buy Me a Coffee sponsor button on the plugin page. No code changes.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Build config cleanup: drop redundant `builtin-modules` npm package in favor of Node's standard `module.builtinModules`. No runtime changes — main.js is byte-identical to 0.1.1.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description: Network usage disclosure in README; no functional changes from 0.1.0.

releases.author: HWY1dot0
releases.description:
Initial release.

- Capture rendered Reading View / Live Preview selection as a PNG to the clipboard
- Theme-aware off-screen render via modern-screenshot
- Desktop only
